verses.text: God recalls Jerusalem's birth: cast out in the open field, unwashed, unpitied, left to die. Helpless beginnings. She owed her very life wholly to the mercy of God.
God recalls Jerusalem's birth: cast out in the open field, unwashed, unpitied, left to die. Helpless beginnings. She owed her very life wholly to the mercy of God.
God recalls Jerusalem's birth: cast out in the open field, unwashed, unpitied, left to die. Helpless beginnings. She owed her very life wholly to the mercy of God.
God recalls Jerusalem's birth: cast out in the open field, unwashed, unpitied, left to die. Helpless beginnings. She owed her very life wholly to the mercy of God.
God recalls Jerusalem's birth: cast out in the open field, unwashed, unpitied, left to die. Helpless beginnings. She owed her very life wholly to the mercy of God.
He passed by and said, 'Live'; He made her flourish, though she was still naked and bare. Sovereign grace. God's first word to the perishing is life undeserved.
He passed by and said, 'Live'; He made her flourish, though she was still naked and bare. Sovereign grace. God's first word to the perishing is life undeserved.
He spread His skirt over her, entered a covenant, and she became His; He washed her clean. A marriage of grace. God took the foundling and made her His own.
He spread His skirt over her, entered a covenant, and she became His; He washed her clean. A marriage of grace. God took the foundling and made her His own.
He clothed her with rich raiment and jewels; she grew 'exceeding beautiful' and rose into a kingdom. Lavish love. All her splendour was His gift, not her own achievement.
He clothed her with rich raiment and jewels; she grew 'exceeding beautiful' and rose into a kingdom. Lavish love. All her splendour was His gift, not her own achievement.
He clothed her with rich raiment and jewels; she grew 'exceeding beautiful' and rose into a kingdom. Lavish love. All her splendour was His gift, not her own achievement.
He clothed her with rich raiment and jewels; she grew 'exceeding beautiful' and rose into a kingdom. Lavish love. All her splendour was His gift, not her own achievement.

Her fame went forth for a beauty 'perfect through my comeliness'; but she trusted in her beauty 'and playedst the harlot.' Gifts misused. She turned God's own adornments into a snare of pride and sin.

Her fame went forth for a beauty 'perfect through my comeliness'; but she trusted in her beauty 'and playedst the harlot.' Gifts misused. She turned God's own adornments into a snare of pride and sin.
She took His garments and gifts to make idols, setting His oil and incense before them. Sacrilege. To use God's blessings in the service of idols is the deepest ingratitude.
She took His garments and gifts to make idols, setting His oil and incense before them. Sacrilege. To use God's blessings in the service of idols is the deepest ingratitude.
She took His garments and gifts to make idols, setting His oil and incense before them. Sacrilege. To use God's blessings in the service of idols is the deepest ingratitude.
She took His garments and gifts to make idols, setting His oil and incense before them. Sacrilege. To use God's blessings in the service of idols is the deepest ingratitude.
She even sacrificed her own children to be devoured, never remembering the day of her helpless youth. Forgotten mercy. Sin grows monstrous when the heart forgets where it came from.
She even sacrificed her own children to be devoured, never remembering the day of her helpless youth. Forgotten mercy. Sin grows monstrous when the heart forgets where it came from.
She even sacrificed her own children to be devoured, never remembering the day of her helpless youth. Forgotten mercy. Sin grows monstrous when the heart forgets where it came from.
She built her high place at every street corner and committed fornication with Egypt and others. Shameless idolatry. The further she went, the more brazen her unfaithfulness.
She built her high place at every street corner and committed fornication with Egypt and others. Shameless idolatry. The further she went, the more brazen her unfaithfulness.
She built her high place at every street corner and committed fornication with Egypt and others. Shameless idolatry. The further she went, the more brazen her unfaithfulness.
She built her high place at every street corner and committed fornication with Egypt and others. Shameless idolatry. The further she went, the more brazen her unfaithfulness.
She played the harlot with Assyria and Chaldea, yet was never satisfied. Insatiable. Sin promises content but only deepens the craving it pretends to fill.
She played the harlot with Assyria and Chaldea, yet was never satisfied. Insatiable. Sin promises content but only deepens the craving it pretends to fill.
She played the harlot with Assyria and Chaldea, yet was never satisfied. Insatiable. Sin promises content but only deepens the craving it pretends to fill.
Unlike a common harlot who is paid, she hires her lovers and gives gifts — 'the contrary is in thee.' Sin upside down. She paid to be ruined, courting evil others would have shunned.
Unlike a common harlot who is paid, she hires her lovers and gives gifts — 'the contrary is in thee.' Sin upside down. She paid to be ruined, courting evil others would have shunned.
Unlike a common harlot who is paid, she hires her lovers and gives gifts — 'the contrary is in thee.' Sin upside down. She paid to be ruined, courting evil others would have shunned.
Unlike a common harlot who is paid, she hires her lovers and gives gifts — 'the contrary is in thee.' Sin upside down. She paid to be ruined, courting evil others would have shunned.
Unlike a common harlot who is paid, she hires her lovers and gives gifts — 'the contrary is in thee.' Sin upside down. She paid to be ruined, courting evil others would have shunned.
Therefore God will gather all her lovers against her and expose her nakedness to them. Sin's shame revealed. The allies she chased become the instruments of her disgrace.
Therefore God will gather all her lovers against her and expose her nakedness to them. Sin's shame revealed. The allies she chased become the instruments of her disgrace.
Therefore God will gather all her lovers against her and expose her nakedness to them. Sin's shame revealed. The allies she chased become the instruments of her disgrace.
He will judge her as women who break wedlock; they shall stone her and burn her houses, and so His fury rest. Covenant broken, judgment falls. Spiritual adultery meets a fearful end.
He will judge her as women who break wedlock; they shall stone her and burn her houses, and so His fury rest. Covenant broken, judgment falls. Spiritual adultery meets a fearful end.
He will judge her as women who break wedlock; they shall stone her and burn her houses, and so His fury rest. Covenant broken, judgment falls. Spiritual adultery meets a fearful end.
He will judge her as women who break wedlock; they shall stone her and burn her houses, and so His fury rest. Covenant broken, judgment falls. Spiritual adultery meets a fearful end.
Then His jealousy shall depart; because she forgot her youth, He repays her ways on her head. Roots exposed. Forgetting God's mercy is itself the root of her doom.
Then His jealousy shall depart; because she forgot her youth, He repays her ways on her head. Roots exposed. Forgetting God's mercy is itself the root of her doom.
Men will say, 'As is the mother, so is her daughter'; her sisters are Samaria and Sodom. Family of sin. Jerusalem's heart matched the very nations she despised.
Men will say, 'As is the mother, so is her daughter'; her sisters are Samaria and Sodom. Family of sin. Jerusalem's heart matched the very nations she despised.
Men will say, 'As is the mother, so is her daughter'; her sisters are Samaria and Sodom. Family of sin. Jerusalem's heart matched the very nations she despised.

She corrupted herself worse than they; Sodom's sin was 'pride, fulness of bread, and abundance of idleness,' and neglect of the poor. A sobering list. Comfort and pride, not only gross vice, provoke God's wrath.

She corrupted herself worse than they; Sodom's sin was 'pride, fulness of bread, and abundance of idleness,' and neglect of the poor. A sobering list. Comfort and pride, not only gross vice, provoke God's wrath.

She corrupted herself worse than they; Sodom's sin was 'pride, fulness of bread, and abundance of idleness,' and neglect of the poor. A sobering list. Comfort and pride, not only gross vice, provoke God's wrath.

She corrupted herself worse than they; Sodom's sin was 'pride, fulness of bread, and abundance of idleness,' and neglect of the poor. A sobering list. Comfort and pride, not only gross vice, provoke God's wrath.
Samaria did not sin half so much; by her greater guilt Jerusalem 'justified' her sisters and must bear her shame. Comparison silenced. The most privileged who sin most are the most guilty.
Samaria did not sin half so much; by her greater guilt Jerusalem 'justified' her sisters and must bear her shame. Comparison silenced. The most privileged who sin most are the most guilty.
Yet God will bring again the captivity of Sodom, Samaria, and her, restoring them to a former estate. Surprising hope. Grace can reach even where judgment seemed final.
Yet God will bring again the captivity of Sodom, Samaria, and her, restoring them to a former estate. Surprising hope. Grace can reach even where judgment seemed final.
Yet God will bring again the captivity of Sodom, Samaria, and her, restoring them to a former estate. Surprising hope. Grace can reach even where judgment seemed final.
In the day of her pride she would not so much as name Sodom; now she bears her own lewdness. Pride humbled. Those who scorned others must own the same shame.
In the day of her pride she would not so much as name Sodom; now she bears her own lewdness. Pride humbled. Those who scorned others must own the same shame.
In the day of her pride she would not so much as name Sodom; now she bears her own lewdness. Pride humbled. Those who scorned others must own the same shame.
He will deal with her as she has done, yet 'I will remember my covenant... and establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.' Grace triumphs. God's faithfulness outlasts His people's unfaithfulness.
He will deal with her as she has done, yet 'I will remember my covenant... and establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.' Grace triumphs. God's faithfulness outlasts His people's unfaithfulness.

She shall remember her ways and be ashamed when He receives her sisters and confirms His covenant, that she may be silent 'when I am pacified toward thee.' Grace humbles. Pardon, not punishment, most deeply breaks a proud heart.

She shall remember her ways and be ashamed when He receives her sisters and confirms His covenant, that she may be silent 'when I am pacified toward thee.' Grace humbles. Pardon, not punishment, most deeply breaks a proud heart.
